title: Mercedes Brings Engine Upgrades to Japan, Says Customer Teams Will Benefit
meta: Published: 22 March 2026 | By Isabella Rossi, Breaking News & Live Reporter | F1CC News
caption: Mercedes has brought performance upgrades to Suzuka.
body:
SUZUKA, JAPAN — Mercedes has arrived at the Japanese Grand Prix with a significant engine upgrade package, the manufacturer confirmed on Saturday. The changes will also be supplied to customer teams McLaren and Williams for the third round of the season.

A Mercedes spokesperson said the modifications target improved performance and should reduce the gap to the front‑running teams. “The upgrades should help bring the competition closer to the front end,” the statement read. “The changes will be available to McLaren and Williams as well.”

The factory Mercedes team enters Suzuka eighth in the constructors’ championship with 28 points, 16 points behind leaders Ferrari. Customer team McLaren sits fifth with 36 points and Williams is seventh with 33. All three Mercedes‑powered squads have trailed Ferrari and the Ford‑powered Red Bull and VCARB cars in straight‑line speed through the opening two rounds.

The upgrades focus on the energy recovery system and combustion efficiency, according to paddock sources familiar with the package. Engineers have worked to improve deployment strategy in race conditions, an area where Mercedes has been losing time relative to Ferrari’s straight‑line performance. The changes are understood to have been accelerated after data from Australia and China showed a deficit of approximately 0.3 seconds per lap on circuits with long full‑throttle sections.

“We have been pushing to bring these updates forward,” one senior Mercedes engineer said, speaking on condition of anonymity. “The correlation between the dyno and the track is good. Suzuka is a high‑speed circuit that will immediately tell us if the step is real.”

The upgrade is the first major power unit development from Brixworth since the 2026 regulations were introduced. All changes have been submitted to the FIA and approved within the regulatory framework. Teams are permitted to introduce performance upgrades to power units during the season under the current homologation rules, provided the modifications are made available to all customer teams simultaneously.

McLaren will receive the upgraded unit for both Zhao Lei and Joshua Nathanial. Team Principal Andrea Stella, reached for comment, said the team welcomed the development. “Mercedes has worked hard on this package. We expect it to give our drivers a better platform, particularly in race trim where we have been losing ground in the later phases of stints.”

Williams will also fit the new specification to both Eren Aygen and Lucas Koopman. The team has been competitive in qualifying but has struggled with degradation on Sundays, a weakness the engine upgrade alone may not solve.

The impact on the competitive order will be measured immediately. Suzuka’s figure‑of‑eight layout punishes power unit deficiencies, with the long run from the hairpin to Spoon Curve and the flat‑out 130R corner revealing every horsepower deficit. First practice begins Friday.

What it means: Mercedes is pushing hard to close the gap after a difficult start. McLaren and Williams — both ahead of the works team in the standings — could be the immediate beneficiaries of a power unit that has more deployment available in the second half of races.

Last updated: 21:45 JST, 22 March 2026

[More to follow]
